commands.review_summary:
The PR changes auto-reply memory-flush error handling so visible flush errors are logged while the main reply proceeds, rotates and clears session state on flush exhaustion, and updates reset/session tests.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +159. Total +166 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main returns visible memory-flush error payloads before the main reply run, and the existing memory-flush runner can emit those payloads on exhaustion. I did not run a live provider-crash reproduction in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed for the transcript-cleanup recovery policy and contributor real-behavior proof; there is no narrow mechanical repair to queue from this read-only review.

target.title: fix: prevent memory flush failure from aborting user reply (#85645)
target.body:
## Summary

When `memoryFlush` fails during the reply pipeline (provider timeout, rate limit, network error), the visible error payload was treated as the final reply via `replyOperation.fail("run_failed", ...)`. This aborted the user's actual message. Since the session remained over threshold, every subsequent message triggered the same flush failure, creating a permanent death loop where no real replies could be delivered.

## Changes

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`**
- Memory flush visible error payloads are now logged via `logVerbose()` and **not** delivered as the reply. The main reply run proceeds normally.
- On flush exhaustion (`memoryFlushFailureCount` crosses `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES` during the current turn), the bloated session is automatically rotated via `resetReplyRunSession()` with `cleanupTranscripts: true`.
- The `resetSession` / `resetSessionAfterRoleOrderingConflict` helpers are hoisted earlier in the function so they are available before the memory flush phase. No behavioral change to their existing callers.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`**
- `initSessionState()` now clears `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, and `memoryFlushLastFailureError` when a session is reset (e.g., via `/new`), preventing stale failure state from carrying over.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts`**
- `resetReplyRunSession()` now clears `compactionCount` (reset to 0) and all `memoryFlush*` fields, ensuring a cleanly rotated session has no lingering flush state.

## What is NOT changed

- `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` behavior is unchanged — it still tracks failures, increments `memoryFlushFailureCount`, and emits lifecycle events (`memory_flush_failed`, `memory_flush_exhausted`).
- `fallbacksOverride: []` is preserved — flush operations still use a fixed model/provider with no fallback chain.
- `MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES = 3` threshold is unchanged.

## Tests

- **`agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`**: Updated existing test to verify flush errors are logged and the main reply continues. Added new test verifying session rotation on flush exhaustion.
- **`agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`**: Added assertions verifying `compactionCount`, `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, `memoryFlushLastFailureError`, and other flush fields are properly cleared on reset.
- **`session.test.ts`**: Added assertions verifying `initSessionState()` clears all memory flush state fields.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Memory flush failure aborting user reply and creating a permanent death loop where every subsequent message produces an error instead of a real reply.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `776738c8587`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The test `"returns source-suppression-safe memory-flush error payloads before the main reply run"` on upstream/main explicitly asserts that flush errors abort the reply with `replyOperation.fail()` and return the error payload as the final response. This is the death loop trigger — the user's message is swallowed and replaced with a maintenance error.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.dedup.test.ts
```
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  160 passed (160)
   Start at  14:53:40
   Duration  11.33s
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The renamed test `"continues the main reply when memory flush reports visible maintenance errors"` confirms flush errors are logged and the main reply proceeds. The new test `"rotates the session when memory flush exhaustion happens during the turn"` confirms session rotation on exhaustion. All 160 tests pass including 55 existing memory flush tests (no regressions).
- **What was not tested**: Live provider failure scenario (tests use mocked `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded`). The embedded flush run visible-error path (where the LLM succeeds but produces a write-restricted error) marks the flush as completed in `agent-runner-memory.ts:1326-1332` regardless of visible errors — this is pre-existing behavior unchanged by this PR and could be addressed as a follow-up.

Closes #85645
target.author: jerry-xin
